Deepwater Bracer  
 


Slot: WRIST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 16
Effect: Stun (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 45
WT: 4.0 Size: SMALL
Class: PAL
Race: ALL except IKS VAH
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Deepwater Bracer

Slot: WRIST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 16
Effect: Stun (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 45
WT: 4.0 Size: SMALL
Class: PAL
Race: ALL except IKS VAH
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Appearance:Plate
Tint:

Stackable:No

Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 50

Old Sebilis
NPC Name
a sebilite golem
frenzied pox scarab - Old Sebilis

Zone Name
Old Sebilis


Uploaded October 5th, 2024 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
Long Distance Stun/Pull
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2002 at 11:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1677457139746
View User Forum
Scholar
78 posts
I have one of these bracers and I LOVE it.. I like to pull using my desist with my Cougar Claw earing (less magic used). But the Desist is only relatively short distance... WELL.. this bracer I am 5,000 miles away (JK) and picking up a stun much farther away than with anyother stun spell I have. So, I like it so far..

As for in battle.. with other tanks in a group, I can chain stun while my lvl 50 Monk and 49 Warrior keeps wacking.. and they can do serious damage while I do this with very little hits back on them.
Okay.. I have Deep Water helm -- LOVE IT
Now... I have Deep Water Bracer - LOVE IT
it is time for the Deep Water Breast Plate -- Gonna LOVE IT.
Still new with this bracer.. testing out on other things too. maybe a stun+DD spell on the undead
Fear+Stun? need to look at it more..
In any case I think I can use the stun for a quick escape if needed.. if out of mana..

Odosan
Human Paladin of the 51st order of
The Knights of Mystic Legend
Bristlebane Server

With the Bazaar zone up, you can pick up this piece for 800 to 1300 pp.
▲ Page top
Eh, some problems
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2002 at 11:22 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966240938
6 posts
I just got this bracer and I have to say I like the AC and the gold look to it, but I got some problems. Casting time on Stun off this baby is what like 6 or 7 secs...when you're in a group and they want you to chain stun the mob, you're swapping 6 seconds of damage for a Stun that doesn't last long at all, most I have ever seen is probably 2-3 second stun, and that's if it doesn't get resisted like it does quite a bit of the time. I'd much rather use the quick-cast Stun we Pallies already have that uses a neglegible amount of mana...so the effect is not quite as effective as it can be. Oh well I enjoy it and it can be used to pull pretty well.
▲ Page top
Chain Stunning
QuoteReply
# Dec 14 2001 at 7:15 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591766944370
8 posts
You can only cast off one bracer at a time, there is a long cast time but no delay to recast. There is no point in having 2 of these for stuns, there is a good reason to have 2 of these which is 16ac bracer.
Chain stunning tip (if you have these spells)

Holyforge, Bracer, Force, Bracer....rinse and repeat. Pending resists the mob will effectively never swing unless you dont cast fast enough or miss your timing. I've done this combo tons of times and works really well at beginning of a fight before mob is slow or throughout a fight if you have no slower.

Hope that answers questions about chain stunning.

Enan
▲ Page top
Re: Chain Casting
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2001 at 8:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591758131109
34 posts
To the person who said they had 2400hp at level 32. Please explain this! I am a 35 paladin and lucky to have 1339 with buffs on.... Is it armor? Or does sta make THAT much of a difference? i have 119 sta. Plz plz plz help me out. I am always getting the crap beat out of me. Thanks
▲ Page top
RE: Re: Chain Casting

# Oct 28 2001 at 1:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591760817587
Sage
148 posts
He meant counting heals and LOH, he effectively has 2500hp before he dies.
▲ Page top
Re: Chain Casting
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 11:56 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Hehe... yes you could chain cast stun with another bracer. and yes right-clickable effects are slower to cast - (because they are mana-free?!?)

I believe this bracer procs at lvl 40 (helm is 45).

In my opinion, I'd rather be fighting then chain casting stun. If you really need to stun the target get gnoll hide lariats and make the pets do the dirty work. Otherwise, all we do is stun and take damage - (you can't hit targets while you're casting).

Oh well, we all have our opinions about class roles...

Draxxar
45th paladin
Xev

Stunning mobs really ticks them off. A pally cannot deal as much damage to a mob as other melee classes, but have other ways to maintain aggro.

# Jul 22 2001 at 11:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757058127
11 posts
this is allways the funniest thing for me when people say pallys can't deal as much dmg who cares my lvl 32 pally has over 2400 hp whit my pally buffs and lay of hands i can take alot of dmg and don't need some one to heal me pallys make great tanks for this reason
just my 2cp
▲ Page top
RE: Re: Chain Casting
QuoteReply
# Feb 25 2002 at 12:50 PM Rating: Good
Ardnahoy
Scholar
340 posts
I've realized that a paladin will never even come close to outdamaging a true melee. But we have mana, so we can bring a lot to a group, just as a chanter can.

My #1 task in a group is to chain-stun caster mobs, and keep the tanks healthy when clerics are low on mana.

Caster popped during battle? No problem: paladin breaks off from tank assist and takes the mean finger-wiggler solo. Stun, run behind and bash, cease, desist, stun, run behind and bash...repeat.

Rogue can't break agro? No problem: flash of light, stun, cease, desist, taunt = one pissed off mob ignoring the rogue.

Warrior getting his **** kicked? No problem: step back, heal the dude a few times, stun mob, step in and bash from behind, taunt, flash of light, cease, taunt, desist, one more taunt, and now you have instant agro on you.

I've maintained agro in a group with a warrior, two monks and a rogue. I also became the main healer in that group when the cleric went LD.

Paladins can do a lot, except outdamaging true melees. We can't even outdamage a sissy little ranger, a vile Shadow Knight, or a well equipped drunkard Bard. So get used to it if you are a paladin; heal, buff, stun, and seconday tank. That is our life.

Edited, Mon Feb 25 12:49:44 2002
____________________________
Duke Ardnahoy
Noble Lord Protector of the 70th Crusade
Fennin Ro - EQ I

Ardnahoy II
Fearless Guardian of the 40th Tour
Mistmore - EQ II

# Aug 13 2001 at 5:17 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
See the difference in "deal damage" and "take damage"?
Ever grouped with a Monk, Rogue or Warrior of your level? Even with my 100+ damage doubleattack (that I have seldom enough) those still outdamage me by far.
And most warriors of your level have 200 to 300 raw HP more than you.
▲ Page top
RE: Re: Chain Casting
QuoteReply
# May 21 2001 at 2:03 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Correction to my last post!

you don't need a second DW bracer... the bracer takes 6 secs to proc and there's no delay!

In other words, right-click - 6 secs to proc - target is stunned up to 4 secs - right-click again :-)

I was grouped a few times today and did nothing but stay back and right-click stun. The groups loved it! Prevented alot of damage! :-)

The bracer has a good range too (was still stunning 100 feet away!)
▲ Page top
RE: Re: Chain Casting
QuoteReply
# Feb 07 2003 at 1:33 AM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
use the word CAST not PROC. proc implies it happens spontaniously wihtout inpout from the player... and yes, it might be confusing to some....

__DEL__1591754889831
3 posts
I know there is a cast time when casting daring with the helm, and it seems longer than normally casting daring, is there a cast time with the bracers? Is it the same time as casting stun? If so, is there a recast time as well and can you use the other bracer or cast stun while the recast time is ticking from the first cast? Sorry about the questions, but would appreciate some info

#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 08 2001 at 1:50 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) How much are these babys? its the best of the arms helm gloves.... i can trade GE boots for this but would that be rip
RE: Cost`
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2001 at 6:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750090920
Scholar
41 posts
Actually, the Helm is probably the best. Manaless heals are always handy and i have personally kept group at full health with no pri healer using just this. The cast time on bracer is very long but can be useful quite often. Just don't expect to be invincible with these right click stuns churning out.
